stimulus: Stretching across what is now Mali, Senegal, and Mauritania, the Mali Empire was one of the wealthiest powers in West Africa between the thirteenth and sixteenth centuries. It ______blank ruled by Mansa Musa from 1312 to 1337.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of verbs to express tense. The past tense verb "was ruled" correctly indicates that Mansa Musa ruled the Mali Empire in the distant past (1312 to 1337). This past tense verb is consistent with the other past tense verb ("was") used to describe the Mali Empire.

Choice B is incorrect because the present tense verb "is ruled" doesn't indicate that Mansa Musa ruled the Mali Empire in the distant past. Choice C is incorrect because the present perfect tense verb "has been ruled" doesn't indicate that Mansa Musa ruled the Mali Empire in the distant past. Choice D is incorrect because the future tense verb "will be ruled" doesn't indicate that Mansa Musa ruled the Mali Empire in the distant past.
